steps: Calculate the annual energy cost savings: $\$4{,}334 - \$2{,}712 = \$1{,}622$ per year.
After $t$ years the total cumulative savings equals $(4{,}334 - 2{,}712) \times t$.

The question asks when savings EXCEED (are strictly greater than) the \$25,000 installation cost, so the inequality is $(4{,}334 - 2{,}712) \times t > 25{,}000$, equivalently $25{,}000 < (4{,}334 - 2{,}712) \times t$.

Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A: $25{,}000 > (4{,}334 - 2{,}712) \times t$ — this says the installation cost exceeds savings, the opposite of what is needed. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $25{,}000 < (4{,}334 - 2{,}712) \times t$ — states that cumulative savings exceed \$25,000, which is exactly the condition required. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: $25{,}000 - 4{,}334 > 2{,}712t$ — incorrectly subtracts the old energy cost from the installation cost and uses only the new cost; this does not represent savings. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — the expression is malformed and does not represent a valid savings inequality. Eliminated.
